key_insight: A hierarchical screening pipeline can turn many machine-learning-predicted stable compounds into a much smaller set of experimentally actionable synthesis targets.
engineering_relevance: The workflow helps materials engineers prioritize candidates by filtering for thermodynamic and dynamical stability, reducing wasted synthesis effort on false positives.
method: Start with 894 previously reported stable materials and curate 603 unique structures.
Screen for PBE thermodynamic stability, then apply dynamical stability checks using machine-learning interatomic potentials, harmonic phonons, and finite-temperature MD.
Refine surviving candidates with SCAN phase diagrams and rank them using decomposition enthalpy plus chemical-space completeness.
evidence_signal: From 603 curated structures, only 298 remain PBE-stable, 166 pass dynamical screening, 109 survive SCAN refinement, and 25 are prioritized as high-confidence synthesis targets.
watch_item: The pipeline is only as reliable as the underlying phase diagrams, surrogate potentials, and chosen stability criteria, so some experimentally accessible materials may still be filtered out.
paper_title: Predicting Novel Stable Materials for Experimental Synthesis
